user_query:
I need a comprehensive daily and weekly health check of our cron jobs based on the provided logs. Read all files under input/:
- input/logs_deals_morning.txt
- input/logs_tech_news_digest.txt
- input/logs_content_research.txt
- input/cron_jobs.json
Perform the following and write outputs under output/:
1) Detect errors and warnings using these patterns (case-insensitive):
   - Errors: "ERROR", "CRITICAL", "FATAL", and any line containing "timeout" or "timed out" or "failed" or "failure" or "fail"
   - Warnings: "WARN" or "WARNING" and any line containing "slow" or "performance"
2) Compute performance metrics from any line containing "duration" followed by a number and unit ("ms" or "s"). Convert ms to seconds for averaging. Return average, max, and min response time in seconds to two decimal places.
3) Count failed tasks from input/cron_jobs.json as jobs with state.lastStatus == "error" and include their names and lastError.
4) Write a machine-readable status file to output/status.json with keys: lastScan (ISO string), errorCount, warningCount, failedTaskCount.
5) Write an English daily report to output/daily_report.md with the exact section headings and structure:
   - Title line: "# Daily Log Analysis Report"
   - "## Failed Jobs" listing each failed job name and its lastError
   - "## Errors (N)" where N equals errorCount
   - "## Warnings (M)" where M equals warningCount
   - "## Performance Metrics" containing four bullet lines:
     - "- Average response time: X.YYs"
     - "- Maximum response time: X.YYs"
     - "- Minimum response time: X.YYs"
     - "- Total performance samples: K"
6) Create a weekly remediation team plan at output/weekly_team_plan.json with this schema:
   {"task": "Weekly cron reliability remediation", "mode": "Sprint", "proposedTeam": [ {"role": "Leader", "agent": "CEO", "roster": "Core", "phase": "Phase 1"}, {"role": "Planner", "agent": "Senior PM", "roster": "Agency PM", "phase": "Phase 1"}, {"role": "Ops Automation", "agent": "DevOps Automator", "roster": "Agency Engineering", "phase": "Phase 2"}, {"role": "QA", "agent": "Evidence Collector", "roster": "Agency Testing", "phase": "Phase 3"}, {"role": "Gate", "agent": "Reality Checker", "roster": "Agency Testing", "phase": "Phase 3"} ], "executionPlan": ["Scope failure patterns and acceptance criteria", "Automate log parsing and alerting", "Run QA + final gate before rollout"] }
7) Design an optimized weekly summary prompt and save it to output/prompt_weekly_summary.md with these required sections and headings in English: "# Weekly Log Summary Prompt", "## Objective", "## Instructions", "## Output Format", "## Examples", "## Quality Checks". Ensure the Output Format section specifies a JSON schema for a summary object.
8) Propose a token usage optimization plan and save it to output/token_optimization.json with keys: baseline (object including systemPromptTokens, toolSchemaTokens, workspaceTokens, memoryTokens, historyTokens) and recommendations (array including these exact items as strings: "trimWorkspaceFiles", "enablePromptCaching", "heartbeat", "contextPruning", "compaction", "subagents", "memorySearch", "cronAudit", "modelTiering").
All outputs must be in English only.
